sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presented with a right knee injury sustained one week ago while trampolining.
He reported hearing a snap and experiencing immediate pain.
He has not sought medical attention until now and has been managing with Tylenol.
He has a history of hypertension and diabetes, and is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily and metformin.
He has not been adherent to his medication regimen and has not checked his blood pressure or blood sugar levels regularly.
He has a inconsistent exercise regimen and an unhealthy diet.
He denies any other symptoms or issues.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and is taking lisinopril 20mg daily and metformin.
He has not been adherent to his medication regimen.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily; Metformin [DOSE] [FREQUENCY] [INDICATION].
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80 mmHg, pulse rate [NOT MENTIONED], oxygen saturation [NOT MENTIONED], temperature [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of his right knee, with a slight limp and decreased range of motion.
The patient's heart has a regular rate and rhythm, and no murmur is appreciated.
His lungs are clear bilaterally.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling related to the lateral ligament strain of the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20mg daily and order an echo to assess for potential medication modification.
I will also order an A1C and lipid panel to assess the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up in one week to discuss the results of the imaging and further management.
